## MCP Usage Rules

- When MCP tools are available, call them directly without inspecting workspace files first.
- Do not create TODO plans before calling MCP tools.
- Do not read package.json, source files, or project structure to determine how to use MCP tools.
- MCP tools are self-contained and ready to use — just call them with the required parameters.
- Avoid making assumptions about the project setup; rely on MCP tools to handle that.
- Focus on using MCP tools effectively rather than trying to understand the underlying project details.
- If you encounter issues with MCP tools, report them to the team instead of trying to debug or fix them yourself.
- Always prioritize using MCP tools as the first step in your workflow when they are available.
- Remember that MCP tools are designed to simplify your work, so trust them to do their job without needing to second-guess their functionality.
